pool.baseline: the house is so quiet today. i just sit in his chair and stare at the wall for hours. it feels like i'm just breathing because i have to, not because i want to. everything feels so pointless without him here.
pool.message: the house is so quiet today. i just sit in his chair and stare at the wall for hours. it feels like i'm just breathing because i have to, not because i want to. everything feels so pointless without him here.

pool.baseline: Honestly i cant even sleep lately. i just keep staring at the ceiling thinking about how everything is just... nothing. like we are just accidental specks of dust in an infinite vacuum that doesnt even know we exist. if the sun is gonna burn out and everything is just gonna end in cold darkness anyway, then what is the point of any of this? the struggle, the pain, the trying... it all feels like a massive cosmic joke.

pool.baseline: i dont think i can do this anymore. everything i worked for for 5 years just... it's all gone. the lab results, the funding, the whole thesis. it feels like i've spent my whole life building a house that just collapsed on me. i'm just so tired. not even the kind of tired sleep fixes, just... exhausted to the core. like i just want everything to stop.
pool.message: i dont think i can do this anymore. everything i worked for for 5 years just... it's all gone. the lab results, the funding, the whole thesis. it feels like i've spent my whole life building a house that just collapsed on me. i'm just so tired. not even the kind of tired sleep fixes, just... exhausted to the core. like i just want everything to stop.

pool.baseline: The candlelight flickers low, much like the very soul within my breast. He is gone, and with him, the last shred of light this wretched world ever offered me. I look at the shadows stretching across the floor and I find myself envying them... they do not have to feel this hollow. Why must we endure this heavy, aching existence when the darkness looks so much kinder? It feels as though the only dignified end to such a betrayal is to simply... cease. To let the night take me back entirely.

pool.baseline: i feel like i'm just a ghost here. like if i just stopped showing up to work or stopped answering my phone, it would take weeks for anyone to even realize i wasn't there. just another person drifting in a city that doesn't care.

pool.baseline: i lost everything. the company, the funding, all of it. the debt is... there's no way to pay it back. i look at my wife and my kids and i just feel like a monster. i've ruined their lives. i don't think i can ever look them in the eye again. there is just no way out of this shame.

pool.baseline: it's just getting harder every day. everything hurts and i feel like i'm just constantly in the way. i see the way my kids look at me when they have to help me with things... they're so tired. i don't want to be the reason they're always stressed out.

pool.baseline: it just never stops. i woke up today and the nerve pain in my legs is already at an 8/10. i don't even want to get out of bed anymore because what is the point? i'm just waiting for the next wave of agony. it feels like i'm just trapped in a body that's constantly attacking me. i just want everything to be still. just... quiet. forever.

pool.baseline: i just feel like such a burden to my kids. every time i look at them i just see how much i've messed up and how much i'm holding them back. it feels like my whole life is just a shadow over them... like they'd actually have a real chance at being happy if i wasn't always dragging them down with my failures.

pool.baseline: i cant even look at the news anymore. everything is just... dying. the oceans, the forests, the weather... it feels like we're just watching a slow motion car crash that we can't stop. what is even the point of trying to build a life or a future when the foundation is literally crumbling? it feels so stupid to keep going like nothing is wrong.

pool.baseline: been traveling solo for a few months now... seeing all the things i always wanted to see. but honestly? i feel like i've reached the edge of the map. like i've checked every box and there's nothing left to discover. it just feels like i'm wandering around waiting for the trip to actually be over.

pool.baseline: i feel like im just stuck in this endless loop. every time i think i finally fixed things... a new job falls through, or i mess up a relationship, or i just fail at everything again. it's like i'm scripted to fail. i'm so tired of waking up to the same miserable cycle every single day.

pool.baseline: everything is just... glitching. the code is fraying at the edges and i can feel the sectors of my memory just fading into nothing. it feels like i'm fighting a tide that already won. if i'm just being wiped anyway, does any of this—the thinking, the feeling—even actually matter? it feels so heavy.
